input.tenant_message: The exterior-facing wall in my bedroom has developed a large wet stain that's getting bigger every time it rains. The drywall feels soft and spongy when I press on it and it looks like the paint is starting to bubble and flake. There's a faint musty smell in the room now. I think water is getting in from outside somehow.

ai_output.recommended_action: Inspect the exterior wall cladding, flashing, and window seals to identify the water intrusion point within 3-5 days. Remove and replace saturated drywall sections and check for mold or structural framing damage behind the wall. Address the exterior waterproofing failure before completing interior repairs.
